methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series B → next-stage step-up multiplier (1.9x) to $20M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.skip_reason: insufficient narrow peer set in Infrastructure & Logistics (found 0 same-stage, 1 adjacent-stage, 0 similar-tier; need ≥3)
methods.rationale: 1 same-sector peers exist in Infrastructure & Logistics, but none of the narrow filters (same-stage / adjacent-stage / similar-valuation-tier) yielded ≥3 datapoints. Falling back to all-sector median would be misleading.

private.name: Earth AI
private.rationale: Same sector (Infrastructure & Logistics), same stage (Series B)

data.narrative:
Amperon is a Series B infrastructure and logistics company that has raised $20M to date. The relevant comp universe in this sector is sparse: two private peers (AiDash, Earth AI) were identified, with no public comparables available. This scarcity constrains the applicability of traditional peer-median methods.

Given insufficient same-stage and adjacent-stage peers to support a robust peer-median analysis, stage-progression methodology was applied as the primary triangulation lens. Using total capital raised ($20M) as a valuation proxy and applying a typical Series B-to-next-stage step-up multiplier of 1.9x yields an implied valuation of $38M. This mechanic reflects observed progression patterns in frontier infrastructure ventures, though it relies on fundraising history rather than direct comparable company metrics.

The analysis supports a valuation range of $32M to $44M (median $38M), with the single-method foundation indicating meaningful uncertainty around this midpoint. Key risks to valuation include the thinness of Infrastructure & Logistics comps at this stage, which limits calibration of appropriate step-up multiples, and the absence of public benchmarks for sector-wide revenue multiples or exit precedents. Additional intelligence on Amperon's revenue run rate, customer concentration, and unit economics would be required to narrow this range materially.
